title: Log model version + timestamp on every sub-agent call for A2A drift detection
description: In agents/shared/sub_agent.py (the module that dispatches calls to specialized agents), capture and log: (1) the model_id returned in the API response metadata, (2) a wall-clock timestamp for each sub-agent invocation, (3) the task type. Write these as structured log lines to logs/. This creates a temporal audit trail so behavioral drift across A2A chains can be detected post-hoc — if the same prompt yields systematically different outputs on different days, the log will show which node drifted and when.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: in an A2A chain, any node undergoing real-time RL (or model updates) has a shifting behavior distribution. 'Same prompt, tomorrow's result may systematically shift.' Mira's super agent dispatches to 8+ specialized agents; if any one node drifts, downstream outputs are silently corrupted. Without a temporal audit trail there is no way to know which node caused instability. This maps directly to Mira's existing Logging and Observability skill: 'log at boundaries and decisions.' A sub-agent call is exactly such a boundary.
diff_description: In sub_agent.py, after receiving each Claude API response, extract response.model (the actual model ID served) and datetime.utcnow().isoformat(). Append a JSON line to logs/a2a_audit.jsonl with fields: {ts, task_type, agent_name, model_id, input_tokens, output_tokens}. No behavior changes — pure observability addition. This is a 5-10 line change around the existing response-handling code.
